topic: Eclipse magnetic ensemble (39 eclipses) — post-EXP-244 cluster canonicalization (Chapman 1919 + Yamazaki & Maute 2017 + Kim & Chang 2018:2040–2049)
our_argument_summary: WIN-068 cites Kim & Chang (2018, Adv. Space Res. 61:2040–2049) 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET ensemble (1991–2016). Verdict: Std Model Explains. The Chapman–Sq framework (Chapman 1919, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218:1–118) with modern conductivity treatment (Yamazaki & Maute 2017, Space Science Reviews 206:299–405) plus the Kim & Chang ensemble explain the observed pattern (Y-component increase, X/Z/F decrease, 30–50° magnetic-latitude peak, ~180-min window) without invoking dome parameters. FSF geometry is explicitly routed to WIN-010/025 where it enters the predicted signal; the 39-eclipse ensemble itself does not invoke FSF. Triple-counting critique (BOU 2017 + 2024 9-station + 1991–2016 ensemble = same Sq mechanism at three aggregation levels) and post-hoc critique (Kim & Chang 2018 published 8 years before V51.0 incorporation) intact.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No WIN-068 monitoring domain present in the dome's currently-published surfaces. raw-text/02-wins.txt only enumerates WINs through WIN-039; WIN-068 is part of the V51 expansion and appears only in evolution/AI-context narrative. The dome no longer publishes monitor.py/pull_data.py source in raw-text — earlier reviews verified by direct grep of historical copies that WIN-068 had no domain block. The 39-eclipse ensemble is a retrospective literature citation; no live validation logic, no API call, no comparison against a dome-derived numerical prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: monitoring = none. Eclipse-related logic in monitor.py (per c8–c14 verifications) targets the 2026 forward projection for WIN-010/025, not the 1991–2016 retrospective ensemble that WIN-068 actually counts.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A — no validation logic to escape from.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our claim that monitor.py has no validation for WIN-068 remains accurate (carried over from c10–c14 verifications; not re-grepped this cycle because dome no longer publishes the script and no claim has changed).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No 39-eclipse ensemble domain block in any currently-published dome source; WIN-068 not enumerated in raw-text/02-wins.txt at all (file only lists through WIN-039). Tag inherited from c10–c14 direct-script verification — not contradicted this cycle.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The phenomenon (Y-component increase, X/Z/F decrease, 30–50° magnetic-latitude peak, ~180-minute window) is explained by the Chapman–Sq framework: Chapman (1919, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218:1–118) for the foundational Sq theory; Yamazaki & Maute (2017, Space Science Reviews 206:299–405) for the modern conductivity treatment. Dome relabels the cause (cavity geometry) without changing the numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Kim & Chang (2018, Adv. Space Res. 61:2040–2049) published ~8 years before V51.0 incorporation (early 2026). No pre-registration of expected values in a venue independent of the author has been located.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Ensemble signal amplitude is extracted from the 1991–2016 INTERMAGNET dataset itself. FSF (which IS dome geometry per the c12 retraction) does not enter this WIN — it is a spectator on the 39-eclipse ensemble and only relevant to the WIN-010/025 2026 forward projection.

holes_found.description: Cluster scope-gap from EXP-244: WIN-025.detail_extra still reads 'Chapman 1933' with a Wikipedia hyperlink to the generic 'Sydney_Chapman_(mathematician)' page. EXP-244's cross_win_consistency_note explicitly promised 'No residual Chapman 1933 references in the Sq context' across the WIN-010/025/068 cluster, but the patch list only enumerated finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_evidence (×3), and detail_verdict_text for WIN-025 — detail_extra was not included. Grep confirms: WIN-010 has 1933=0, WIN-068 has 1933=0, but WIN-025 has 1933=1 (the leftover detail_extra mention). This is a sibling-WIN issue, not a WIN-068 issue per se, but it matters for WIN-068 because (a) the c14 H1 finding graded the cluster-wide inconsistency MAJOR specifically because WIN-068's text invites readers to follow #win010/#win025 anchors, (b) EXP-244 was framed as a coordinated cluster patch that explicitly pre-empts that defender wedge, and (c) the residual is now a single one-occurrence stub rather than a 12-field divergence — so the wedge is narrower but the EXP's own consistency promise is still falsified. Severity downgraded from c14 H1 'major' to 'moderate' to reflect ~95% successful cluster fix; not 'minor' because the EXP's explicit promise was the most-checkable part of the patch and it failed at the one spot the patch list didn't enumerate. NOTE: the linked Wikipedia URL anchors to the mathematician's biography page, not to a 1933 paper, so the citation is also dangling — neither the year nor the reference target is correct for the Sq foundational citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Tiny one-line fix at WIN-025.detail_extra: replace the substring 'Chapman 1933' (and its Wikipedia link target) with 'Chapman 1919' linking to the same Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218 DOI used elsewhere in the cluster (10.1098/rsta.1919.0001), or simply 'Chapman (1919)' bare to match the surrounding cluster prose. Decider can self-apply; no analyst expansion needed. Additionally, the EXP-244 patch-author workflow lesson: the cross_win_consistency_note in future cluster patches should be validated by an automated grep of the post-patch tree for the deprecated string. A 5-line node script (count occurrences of 'Chapman 1933' across the cluster fields, expect 0) would have caught this pre-commit.

holes_found.description: EXP-244 dropped the inline hyperlink for Yamazaki & Maute in WIN-068.detail_evidence ¶3. Pre-EXP, the prose said 'modern conductivity treatment by Yamazaki & Maute (2021)' with the (incorrect) Frontiers URL inline. Post-EXP, the bare prose 'modern conductivity treatment by Yamazaki & Maute (2017, Space Science Reviews 206:299–405)' appears in two places in detail_evidence — only one of which (the parenthetical near the Sq mechanism description) carries the new DOI link to 10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z. The earlier in-text mention of Y&M 2017 in the same paragraph is bare. Defender wedge: 'where's the link?' Easy fix; cosmetic-to-citation-completeness. The c14 citations_verified entry can stand for the linked instance, but the bare instance reduces click-through verifiability.
holes_found.recommendation: Add an inline hyperlink to https://doi.org/10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z on the first 'Yamazaki & Maute (2017)' mention in detail_evidence, matching the pattern already used in the Sq-mechanism sentence later in the same paragraph. Decider self-apply. Optionally: consolidate to a single mention with the link, since two mentions of the same citation in the same paragraph reads slightly redundant — but only do this if the de-duplication preserves the c14-validated claim chain.

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict awkward phrasing carried over from c13/c14: 'documented since Chapman (1919) and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018'. Reads ambiguously — 'since [the paper] Chapman (1919)' instead of 'since Chapman's 1919 work'. EXP-244 W068-TV1 was a no-op in practice (the 'rastogi-removal-if-present' patch was a defensive idempotent because tldr_verdict didn't carry Rastogi anyway), so this c14 H3 phrasing critique persists unchanged. Not factually wrong; optics-only.
holes_found.recommendation: Next touch of tldr_verdict, rephrase to 'documented since Chapman's 1919 work' or 'documented across the Chapman–Sq literature since 1919.' No urgency — meaning is clear. Could ride on any future TLDR refresh.

holes_found.description: verdict_history (carried through c12–c14): two-flip sequence from 2026-04-12 (Std→Self-Contradicted via EXP-078) and 2026-04-13 (Self-Contradicted→Std Model Explains via curmudgeon gate-2 analysis) is preserved without a stability_note field. Decider has consistently ranked this low-priority auditability noise. Not re-opening; noting only for completeness.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Optional future enhancement: add verdict_history[i].stability_note schema field across all WINs in a single coordinated infrastructure change.

stronger_arguments.description: Advocate-mode steel-man for the dome defender: the strongest counter to our 'Std Model Explains' verdict is that WIN-068 is paired with PROS-003 ('Eclipse Magnetic Anomaly Tracks Geometry'), which qualitatively predicts an electromagnetic-aetheric (not thermal/gravitational) signal that follows the umbra path rather than local solar time. That qualitative class-claim IS confirmed by the 39-eclipse ensemble. Defenders can argue we're treating WIN-068 unfairly by demanding numerical discrimination when the dome's prediction was a class-discrimination (EM not gravity, geometry not clock). This is the steel-manned argument we should pre-empt. Counter-counter: (a) 'EM not gravity' is also predicted by Chapman–Sq from first principles; the dome did not predict EM-vs-gravity discrimination uniquely. (b) 'Geometry not clock' is the well-known Sq-vs-LT distinction in the standard literature. (c) A class-claim that is also predicted by the standard model is, by construction, non-discriminating — which is exactly the verdict we hold. Worth adding 1-2 sentences acknowledging the class-claim and dispatching it, rather than letting a defender spring it as a gotcha.
sources: Chapman 1919 (general Sq derivation predicts shadow-tracking not LT-tracking)

stronger_arguments.description: EXP-244 surgical-patch workflow lesson worth promoting: cluster patches that touch ~6 named fields per WIN across 3 sibling WINs (~18 substring substitutions) need a post-apply verification grep of the deprecated string across the entire cluster, not just the enumerated fields. EXP-244's cross_win_consistency_note made an explicit factual claim ('No residual Chapman 1933 references in the Sq context') that was false at apply-time because the patch list didn't enumerate detail_extra in WIN-025. A 5-line grep gate would have caught this. Worth memorializing as a decider/analyst checklist item: after any cluster citation patch, grep the deprecated string across all cluster files; if grep finds anything, either extend the patch list or weaken the consistency note.
sources: c13/c14 H1 escalation logic that EXP-244 was designed to extinguish

stronger_arguments.description: Triple-counting aggregate-tally idea (carried from c13 stronger_arguments #2 and c14 stronger_arguments #2): a single cluster-level table or sidebar showing 'dome's claimed WIN count → deduplicated count' is still unbuilt. If implemented, the WIN-025.detail_extra residual flagged in hole #1 would have been caught by whoever drafted the table (they would have to reconcile citations across the cluster to write coherent prose). Absence is now actively enabling consistency lapses across multiple cycles. Suggest escalating the c13/c14 suggestion to a moderate finding next cycle if EXP-244-style cluster patches keep finding residuals.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-244 is genuinely good citation work. Three improvements: (1) Chapman 1919 is the correct foundational Sq paper (Chapman 1933 was about lunar tidal magnetism, not solar-eclipse Sq — c13 caught this and EXP-244 fixed it cluster-wide). (2) Yamazaki & Maute 2017 (Space Science Reviews 206:299–405, DOI 10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z) IS the canonical modern Sq review — the prior Frontiers DOI (10.3389/fspas.2021.766327) actually resolved to Meza et al. 2021 (Patagonian eclipse), a different paper entirely. EXP-244 caught a real DOI-resolution failure that the c13 citation_check had marked verified. (3) Kim & Chang 2018 page range is now accurate (2040–2049, ADS-confirmed). The EXP also correctly DROPPED Rastogi 1962 as unverifiable rather than propagate an ambiguous family-reference. This is the right kind of citation hygiene.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right about citation canonicalization at one or two WINs in the cluster doesn't change the underlying scientific verdict — Std Model Explains stands regardless of which year's paper Chapman published the Sq framework. The post-hoc and non-discriminating critiques are independent of citation accuracy. The kernel here is that we got better at being right (citation cleanup), not that the dome got more right. The remaining detail_extra scope-gap is also citation hygiene, not a substance change.

deeper_analysis:
Step 5 staleness check: ran git pull --rebase against origin/main; HEAD now at 31fe2ba (workspace sync 2026-04-25T04:01:59Z). Most recent decider commits (e56111a, fd1d37e, 61451cf) since EXP-244 apply (7b2fe22) touch WIN-067 SAA citation work, not the Chapman cluster. WIN-068 content is stable since 7b2fe22 — finding/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/tldr_verdict all reflect post-EXP-244 state.

Verification grep results (Chapman / Yamazaki / Kim&Chang citation consistency post-EXP-244):
  WIN-010: Chapman=11, 1919=6, 1933=0, Yamazaki=4, 2017=16, 2021=0, Rastogi=0, KC pages 2049=1/2054=0
  WIN-025: Chapman=9,  1919=6, 1933=1, Yamazaki=3, 2017=7,  2021=0, Rastogi=0, KC pages 2049=1/2054=0
  WIN-068: Chapman=9,  1919=4, 1933=0, Yamazaki=4, 2017=10, 2021=0, Rastogi=0, KC pages 2049=2/2054=0
  Meza-DOI (766327)=0 cluster-wide (good — incorrect DOI fully removed)
  Y&M-2017 DOI (0282-z)=1 in WIN-068 (linked once in detail_evidence)

EXP-244 successfully scrubbed Rastogi, Y&M-2021, and Meza-DOI cluster-wide. Cluster Chapman-1919 consistency is 95% complete: only WIN-025.detail_extra has the lingering 'Chapman 1933' (with a Wikipedia URL pointing to the mathematician's bio page rather than to a 1933 paper). This is the H1 finding. Severity grading reasoning, per the 'consider the other side' brief: a defender skilled enough to grep for inconsistencies will see the leftover; a casual reader following the cross-reference path from WIN-068 → WIN-025 will not see detail_extra unless they expand it (it's behind a UI disclosure). On the lower-severity side: detail_extra is collapsed-by-default, the deprecated Wikipedia link is generic enough to not be a load-bearing citation, and the EXP fix path is trivial (one substring replacement). On the higher-severity side: the EXP author wrote a consistency-note that is now publicly false at one location, and the author/curmudgeon promised this exact bug would be fixed cluster-wide. I land on 'moderate' — neither the major-grade defender wedge of the c14 H1 era (12 fields × 2 WINs of visible mismatch) nor a minor optics issue (the EXP's promise WAS specifically about exactly this string and this location).

advocate-mode and cross-WIN consistency lenses produced stronger_arguments #1 (PROS-003 class-claim steel-man) and #2 (EXP workflow lesson). Quantitative-verification lens: I confirmed the Yamazaki & Maute 2017 DOI 10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z resolves to Springer Space Science Reviews vol 206 — the citation venue/volume/page-range trio is internally consistent. Did not externally re-fetch the DOI this cycle (carrying c14 verification), but the structure (Springer SpringerLink DOI prefix 10.1007, journal s11214 = Space Science Reviews, year/volume 016/206 — Springer's encoding) is consistent with the named venue. Kim & Chang page range 2040–2049 matches the ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K (carried from EXP-244 verification, not externally re-checked).

Verdict (Std Model Explains) is sound and unchanged. code_analysis_tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) are unchanged and remain accurate post-EXP-244. WIN-068 is in good internal shape; the residual cluster issue is in WIN-025, not WIN-068, so recommended_action for WIN-068 is no_change. Hole #1 is a flag for the decider to apply a tiny WIN-025 patch — not a WIN-068 rewrite trigger.

PROP-009 assertion: queue_id set to 257, matches queue item target_id=WIN-068 pushed_at 2026-04-24T19:26:29.175Z. trigger=priority-queue-win-rewrite (matches target_type='win-detail-rewrite' from queue).

citations_verified: Chapman (1919) Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218:1–118 — foundational Sq theory; canonical citation post-EXP-244 in WIN-068 finding/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/tldr_verdict (1919=4 occurrences)
Kim & Chang (2018) Adv. Space Res. 61:2040–2049 — ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K page range corrected post-EXP-244 (was 2040–2054)

Yamazaki & Maute (2017) Space Science Reviews 206:299–405, DOI 10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z — corrected from prior Y&M 2021 / Meza 2021 DOI mis-attribution post-EXP-244; canonical modern Sq conductivity review
citations_failed: WIN-025.detail_extra still references 'Chapman 1933' with a generic Wikipedia link to 'Sydney_Chapman_(mathematician)' — this is in the sibling WIN, not WIN-068, but it falsifies EXP-244's cross_win_consistency_note 'no residual Chapman 1933 references in the Sq context' for the cluster as a whole. Flagged in hole #1 for decider self-apply.
citations_unchecked: FSF formula r·tan(θ) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) — carried from WIN-010 review; not re-verified this cycle
V51.0 release date (early 2026, used for 8-year post-hoc gap) — not externally verified; carried from prior cycles

change_summary: EXP-244 (commit 7b2fe22) integrated 2026-04-24T19:28Z. Effect on WIN-068: (1) 'Yamazaki & Maute 2021' (incorrect — DOI resolved to Meza et al. 2021 Patagonian eclipse paper) replaced with 'Yamazaki & Maute (2017) Space Science Reviews 206:299–405' with corrected DOI 10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z; (2) Rastogi 1962 (unverifiable as eclipse-specific) dropped from finding and detail_evidence; (3) Kim & Chang page range corrected 2040–2054 → 2040–2049; (4) detail_evidence Chapman intro tightened to specify Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218:1–118. Cluster propagation to WIN-010 and WIN-025 (Chapman 1933 → Chapman 1919) is 95% complete — single residual at WIN-025.detail_extra (1 instance of 'Chapman 1933' with a Wikipedia bio-link), flagged in hole #1. Triple-counting argument, FSF carve-out, post-hoc argument, and verdict are unchanged from c14.
